text: T-021 eval. A popular workflow (Jack Roberts) builds client websites with Claude + design inspiration (Dribbble) + Firecrawl (scrape/understand reference sites) + Kie.ai (image gen) + Vercel deploy + a CMS dashboard (clients self-edit copy/images/SEO) + MongoDB + secure client handoff (you keep master control). We (ONE TEN) already do Claude-build + Vercel-deploy. Produce tasks/T-021/EVAL.md: what's genuinely worth adopting? Focus on (a) the CMS / client-self-edit / HANDOFF layer = the piece that turns our one-off micro-sites into a SCALABLE agency (how to implement: custom vs headless CMS like Sanity/Payload/Decap/TinaCMS, Vercel-fit), and (b) design-reference sourcing (Dribbble + Firecrawl) to raise UI polish. Give a concrete adopt recommendation. Commit + warroom-say.
